seo.title: Tirzepatide in Australia — Mounjaro TGA Registration, PBS Access and Cost
seo.description: TGA-registered tirzepatide in Australia. How Mounjaro is prescribed for type 2 diabetes, what PBS subsidies apply, what private prescriptions cost, and what TGA advertising rules mean for patients.

trust.disclaimer: This page summarises Australian tirzepatide access based on publicly available TGA, PBS, and government data. Regulatory status, PBS listings, and prescribing rules change regularly. Verify current availability and eligibility with a registered Australian prescriber or directly via the TGA and PBS websites.

marketSummary: In Australia, tirzepatide is TGA-registered as Mounjaro for type 2 diabetes management and listed on the PBS for eligible patients. As of this review, tirzepatide is not separately TGA-registered for chronic weight management in Australia. The Zepbound brand (the US weight management product) does not hold a distinct TGA registration. Prescribers may use tirzepatide off-label for weight management at their clinical discretion.
approvalScope: Type 2 diabetes (Mounjaro) — TGA registered and PBS listed

approvedProducts.name: Mounjaro
approvedProducts.summary: Tirzepatide subcutaneous injection TGA-registered for type 2 diabetes management in adults. PBS listed for eligible T2D patients, providing subsidised access under the Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme.

costSummary: PBS-eligible T2D patients access Mounjaro at the standard PBS patient contribution — substantially lower than private prescription cost. Private prescriptions for T2D or off-label weight management use typically range from AUD $300 to $650 per month depending on dose and pharmacy. Consultation fees are additional.
providerPathway: Australian patients access tirzepatide through a GP or endocrinologist who can write a PBS prescription for eligible T2D patients. Private prescribing for weight management is available from some GPs and obesity medicine specialists. Medicare-rebated telehealth consultations are available for patients who meet the telehealth eligibility criteria.
onlinePharmacyNotes: Online prescribing and dispensing is available through AHPRA-registered practitioners and pharmacies. Patients should verify that any online prescriber is registered with AHPRA and that dispensed products are TGA-registered therapeutic goods, not unapproved imports or compounded alternatives.
legalNotes: Tirzepatide (Mounjaro) requires a valid Australian prescription from an AHPRA-registered prescriber. Purchasing without a prescription is illegal.
The Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 prohibits advertising prescription-only medicines to the general public. This applies to tirzepatide the same way it applies to semaglutide and other GLP-1 medicines.

Tirzepatide for weight management is currently off-label in Australia and PBS subsidy does not apply to non-T2D indications. A separate TGA registration would be required before an approved weight management pathway could exist.

faqs.question: Is tirzepatide approved for weight loss in Australia?
faqs.answer: As of this review, tirzepatide is TGA-registered in Australia only for type 2 diabetes under the Mounjaro brand. The Zepbound brand (tirzepatide for weight management, FDA-approved in the US) does not hold a separate TGA registration. Prescribers may use Mounjaro off-label for weight management at their discretion, but this is not PBS-subsidised.

faqs.question: Can I get Mounjaro on the PBS in Australia?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you have type 2 diabetes and meet the PBS listing criteria. Speak to your GP or endocrinologist, who will assess your eligibility and write a PBS prescription if appropriate. Patients outside the T2D PBS criteria will pay private prescription prices.

faqs.question: How much does Mounjaro cost privately in Australia?
faqs.answer: Private Mounjaro prescriptions in Australia typically cost AUD $300–$650 per month depending on dose and pharmacy. PBS-eligible T2D patients pay the standard PBS patient contribution, which is significantly lower. Consultation fees are charged separately by most providers.

faqs.question: Can I order Mounjaro online in Australia?
faqs.answer: Yes, from AHPRA-registered practitioners and TGA-approved pharmacies. The TGA monitors and acts against suppliers of unapproved or counterfeit therapeutic goods. Verify that any service you use employs an AHPRA-registered prescriber and uses a Pharmacy Board-registered dispensary.

cta.notes: Australian provider routing and cost tools are in development. Join the waitlist to be notified when AU-specific features launch.

sources.title: Australian Register of Therapeutic Goods — Mounjaro (tirzepatide)
